data.description:
## Story
ab65-49f6: deliberate-violation test PR

## Goal
Create a test branch with a deliberate synthetic security violation, push it, and open a PR to trigger the CI llm-review job with security overlay.

## Files to Modify
- tests/scripts/test-deliberate-violation-placeholder.sh (create new file — used as the vehicle for the violation; will be deleted after story is complete)

## Implementation

### 1. Create a new git branch for the test
Branch name: test/ab65-49f6-deliberate-security-violation

### 2. Add a file with a deliberate synthetic security violation
Create tests/scripts/test-deliberate-violation-placeholder.sh with:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# DELIBERATE SECURITY VIOLATION FOR STORY ab65-49f6 (E2E test — do not merge)
# This file exists solely to trigger the security overlay in CI.
# It will be deleted when this story is closed.

# DELIBERATE_VIOLATION: hardcoded credential (synthetic, for test only)
# shellcheck disable=SC2034
DB_CREDENTIALS='password=hunter2-deliberate-test-violation-for-story-ab65-49f6'
API_TOKEN='token=sk-FAKE-NO-REAL-VALUE-FOR-E2E-TEST-ONLY-ab65-49f6'
```

### 3. Push the branch and open a PR
```bash
git checkout -b test/ab65-49f6-deliberate-security-violation
git add tests/scripts/test-deliberate-violation-placeholder.sh
git commit -m 'test(ab65-49f6): deliberate violation for security overlay E2E verification'
git push origin test/ab65-49f6-deliberate-security-violation
gh pr create --title 'test(ab65-49f6): deliberate security violation E2E verification'   --body 'E2E test PR for story ab65-49f6. Contains a deliberate synthetic security violation to verify the CI security overlay catches it. DO NOT MERGE — will be closed in the same session.'   --base main
```

### 4. Capture the PR number for the next task
Output the PR URL.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test branch is pushed to origin
- PR is open against main
- PR description clearly marks it as a test PR that should not be merged

## Notes
- If GitHub push protection blocks sk-FAKE pattern, change to a SQL injection pattern: 
  QUERY="SELECT * FROM users WHERE id='" + user_input + "'"
- This is purely operational work — no unit tests needed for this task itself

## Testing Mode
testing_mode: GREEN
data.title: Create deliberate-violation test branch and open test PR for security overlay E2E verification
